title: Kashmiri Alphabets – Devanagari Alphabets
content: Kashmiri is written in the Devanagari script (same as Hindi). Learn vowels (स्वर) and consonants (व्यंजन) below.
intro: Kashmiri uses the Devanagari script, which is also used for Hindi, Marathi, and Kashmiri. Below are the vowels and consonants with transliteration and example words.

title: Vowels, Consonants in Kashmiri and their pronunciation
content: Pronunciation of Kashmiri vowels and consonants. Kashmiri has eight vowel phonemes; length can be distinctive in Sanskrit-derived words.
intro: Each letter in Kashmiri Devanagari represents a consistent sound. Vowels can be short or long; nasalization is common. Use the speaker to hear pronunciation.

blocks.heading: Vowel pronunciation
blocks.content: Front: इ (i), ई (ī). Central: अ (a), आ (ā). Back: उ (u), ऊ (ū), ओ (o), ए (e). In many Kashmiri words, vowel length is often not phonemic; in Sanskrit-derived words it can change meaning (e.g. दिन 'day' vs दीन 'destitute').

title: Kashmiri Barakhadi: Symbols for vowels with consonants
content: How vowels combine with consonants in Kashmiri Devanagari. Vowel signs (matras) are attached to consonant symbols.
intro: In Devanagari, the vowel अ (a) is inherent in a consonant; other vowels are shown with matras (e.g. ा for आ, ि for इ, ी for ई, ु for उ, ू for ऊ, े for ए, ै for ऐ, ो for ओ, ौ for औ).

blocks.heading: Matra examples
blocks.content: क + आ = का (kā); क + इ = कि (ki); क + ई = की (kī); क + उ = कु (ku); क + ऊ = कू (kū); क + ए = के (ke); क + ऐ = कै (kai); क + ओ = को (ko); क + औ = कौ (kau). Same pattern applies to all consonants.

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Kashmiri (Devanagari)
content: Common pronunciation pitfalls when reading Kashmiri in Devanagari. Kashmiri has some sounds that differ from Hindi.
intro: Learners familiar with Hindi may notice differences in vowel length, nasalization, and a few consonant sounds. Practice with the sentences below.

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.content: Kashmiri has two varieties of /e/, /o/, /ai/ and /au/ (long and short) that are not always distinguished in the script. Nasalization (चाँद, काँच) is common. The grapheme ऐ can represent /ai/, /æ/, or /aĕ/ depending on the word.

title: Pronunciation of Anusvar (अनुस्वार) in Kashmiri
content: The anusvar (ं) is a dot above the letter indicating nasalization. In Kashmiri it can represent ङ, ञ, ण, न, or म depending on the following consonant.
intro: अं is written as a dot (ं) above the consonant. It nasalizes the vowel and often assimilates to the place of the next consonant (e.g. अंग 'body', संग 'with').

title: Combining consonants in Kashmiri (कॉशुर जोडाक्षरे)
content: Consonant clusters (जोडाक्षर) in Kashmiri. The halant (्) removes the inherent 'a' so two consonants can join.
intro: When two consonants come together, the first is often written with a halant (्) so that no vowel sits between them (e.g. क् + त = क्त).

title: Combining consonants with र (r) – Kashmiri जोडाक्षरे Part 2
content: How र (r) combines with other consonants: as र् before a consonant (reph) or as a subscript (e.g. क्र, प्र, त्र).
intro: र can appear above the line (reph) in clusters like र्क, र्म, or as a subscript in क्र (kra), प्र (pra), त्र (tra), etc.

title: Combining consonants with ह (h) – Kashmiri जोडाक्षरे Part 3
content: Combinations with ह (h): ध, भ, झ, घ, etc., and conjuncts like ह्न, ह्म, ह्र.
intro: ह appears in many conjuncts (e.g. क्ष, which is क्+ष, and words like प्रेम, धन्यवाद). Aspirated consonants (ख, घ, छ, झ, थ, ध, फ, भ) use a puff of breath.

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants in Kashmiri
content: Special conjuncts in Devanagari: क्ष (kṣa), त्र (tra), ज्ञ (jña). These have their own shapes in Kashmiri.
intro: Some consonant clusters have dedicated symbols: क्ष (ksha), त्र (tra), ज्ञ (jña). They are used in Sanskrit-derived and common Kashmiri words.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Kashmiri
